verdict_reasoning: ABCB9 (TAPL) is a lysosomal ABC transporter that resides on the lysosomal/late-endosomal membrane, translocating peptides from the cytosol into the lysosomal lumen. Its primary steady-state localization is the lysosomal membrane, with the peptide-binding domains facing the cytoplasm and the transport channel opening into the lysosomal lumen. Checking all contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented stress/activation-induced PM translocation; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no lineage-specific PM display reported; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — while lysosomes can fuse with PM in specialized contexts, no specific evidence that ABCB9-positive lysosomes do so in a therapeutically relevant manner; (4) dual_localization — no documented steady-state PM pool in surface proteomics or flow cytometry data; (5) stable_surface_attachment — secreted form not known. No therapeutic antibody/ADC programs targeting cell-surface ABCB9 are documented. The protein is an endomembrane-resident transporter confined to lysosomal membranes.
